text: The average member of Congress was born closer to the first ballpoint pen sold in America than the iPhone.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The first ballpoint pen sold in America: October 29, 1945 (TIME; Reynolds Rocket at Gimbels, New York). The iPhone: January 9, 2007 (Apple Newsroom).

text: The average member of Congress was born closer to the launch of Sputnik than the iPhone.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The launch of Sputnik: October 4, 1957 (U.S. State Department, Office of the Historian). The iPhone: January 9, 2007 (Apple Newsroom).

text: The average member of Congress was born closer to the invention of the transistor than the launch of Facebook.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The invention of the transistor: December 16, 1947 (Computer History Museum). The launch of Facebook: February 4, 2004 (History of Facebook (Wikipedia)).

text: The average member of Congress was born closer to the founding of NASA than the founding of Google.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The founding of NASA: July 29, 1958 (NASA History Office). The founding of Google: September 4, 1998 (EDN; California incorporation records).

text: The average member of Congress was born closer to the polio vaccine announcement than the fall of the Berlin Wall.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The polio vaccine announcement: April 12, 1955 (University of Michigan School of Public Health). The fall of the Berlin Wall: November 9, 1989 (U.S. State Department, Office of the Historian).

text: The average member of Congress was 3 years old when Apollo 11 landed on the Moon.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The Moon landing: July 20, 1969 (NASA).

text: The average member of Congress was 3 years old when the first ARPANET message was sent.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The first Internet message: October 29, 1969 (UCLA Kleinrock Internet History Center).

text: The average member of Congress was 23 years old when the Berlin Wall fell.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The fall of the Berlin Wall: November 9, 1989 (U.S. State Department, Office of the Historian).

text: The average member of Congress was 25 years old when the World Wide Web was announced to the public.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The public debut of the World Wide Web: August 6, 1991 (CERN).

text: The average member of Congress was 32 years old when Google was incorporated.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The founding of Google: September 4, 1998 (EDN; California incorporation records).

text: The average member of Congress was 37 years old when Facebook launched.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The launch of Facebook: February 4, 2004 (History of Facebook (Wikipedia)).

text: The average member of Congress was 40 years old when the iPhone was announced.
footnote: Based on the mean birth date of the 530 voting members of Congress: June 1, 1966. The iPhone: January 9, 2007 (Apple Newsroom).
